front: Philosopher: leading Academic skeptic who argued that although there are no cognitive impressions, knowledge is still possible through probabilistic reasoning
Philosopher: rival of Chrysippus who apocryphally said 'without Chrysippus, there would be no me'
Philosopher: names a 'plank' thought experiment used to discuss self-defense killings involving two shipwrecked sailors
work: Plank of Carneades and Rome Visit

front: Philosopher: gave contradictory lectures on justice while visiting Rome, arguing for it one day and against it the next
work: Plank of Carneades and Rome Visit
